title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: This podcast episode explores whether AI systems are vulnerable to social engineering attacks similar to humans, discusses a self-replicating AI worm from University of Toronto that can navigate networks autonomously, and examines the Sophos State of Identity Security 2026 report finding that nonhuman identities contribute significantly to identity-based data breaches.
writeup: This IBM Technology podcast episode covers three AI security threats: whether AI systems can be manipulated through social engineering and prompt injection to reveal secrets, a self-replicating AI worm developed at the University of Toronto, and the role of nonhuman identities in identity-based data breaches per Sophos research. The discussion matters because AI agents and the credentials they hold expand the attack surface beyond traditional human-centered defenses. Key takeaways: prompt injection can coax AI into divulging protected information, autonomous worms can propagate across AI systems, and nonhuman identities account for a significant share of identity-based breaches, raising the stakes for practices like credential rotation.
